other_observers: Laurie Duke and two others from Denver Audubon; I found and identified the bird for the group.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, just south of the large willow at the edge of the Banding Station along a spring-fed canal.
description: This was the largest bird in the flock that was feeding in some weeds by the edge of a narrow canal with water.  The bird was overall reddish in coloration. The tail was red, the back was gray with with some red streaks. The face had a large red auricular patch. The bill was yellowish. The breast was white with wide reddish streaks extending to the flanks. There was a wide reddish stickpin on the chest. The wings were reddish. The supercilium was wide and gray. The breast and flank streaks were orderly and looked like arrowhheads, largest on the flanks. The nape was gray.Behaviors: Fed with mixed-species flock on the ground, flying up to perch when the group got close.Call: distinctive call alerted us to the birdPlumage: unknown
